items.text: gap.py writes new records as .lume/gaps/<source>-G<nnnn>-<stub>.json: numeric id zero-padded to 4 in the FILENAME only (the gap id stays 'G<n>' and the (source,id) dedupe identity is unchanged), <stub> slugified from the title and length-capped; read_gaps still reads every *.json so any legacy name remains readable

check.cmd: cd .lume/gaps && ls *.json | grep -vqE '^[a-z0-9_.-]+-G[0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9-]+\.json$' && exit 1 || ls *.json | wc -l | grep -qx 9

items.text: All 9 existing records under .lume/gaps/ are migrated to the new filenames via git mv (history-preserving); no legacy <source>-G<n>.json name remains

check.cmd: python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_gap.py tests/test_gap_scan.py 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | grep -q passed

items.text: add/list/scan/link/resolve all work against the new naming: the adopter-scan ingest path writes ingested gaps with the new filename too, and (source,id) addressing (gap link/resolve lume G2 etc.) is unaffected

check.cmd: grep -q 'G<nnnn>-<stub>' plugin/README.md && grep -q 'G<nnnn>-<stub>' plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md && ! grep -rn '<source>-<id>.json' plugin/README.md plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md

items.text: Docs stating the old convention are updated: plugin/README.md and plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md describe <source>-G<nnnn>-<stub>.json; no doc still says gaps live at <source>-<id>.json
Tests assert the new filename shape (a written record lands at the padded, stubbed path; a legacy-named record is still read); full suite green with no assertion weakened
Loop residue (0018 accept/close flips, the three new workstreams 0019-0021, gap links G2-G7) is committed; .lume/gaps and .lume/reviews clean at handback

check.cmd: python3 -c "import json,glob; f=glob.glob('.lume/gaps/lume-G0007-*.json'); assert f, 'no new-format G7 file'; d=json.load(open(f[0])); assert d['status']=='resolved' and d['resolution']['kind']=='implemented' and '0019' in d['workstreams']; print('ok')"

items.text: Dogfood: G7 is resolved through gap resolve -w gap-file-naming with a structured resolution, and its own record sits at the new-format filename

dod.preamble: Answer G7: gap filenames follow the workstream NNNN-stub convention, adapted for the multi-source store as <source>-G<nnnn>-<stub>.json. Filename-only change: ids and the (source,id) identity are untouched.

handback: Gap filenames now follow the workstream convention: <source>-G<nnnn>-<stub>.json. All 9 records migrated history-preservingly, docs and tests updated, legacy names still read and self-migrate on next write, suite 417 green, G7 dogfood-resolved by 0019 with its record at the new-format name. One addition beyond the DoD's letter, disclosed in the self-review: rename-on-write cleanup so a legacy file never coexists with its renamed successor.
self_review: All seven DoD checks pass machine-verified (0 failed / 7); suite 417 passed (one test added, one reshaped for the new filename). The change is filename-only as specified: _filename pads the numeric id to 4 and appends a slugified, 40-char-capped title stub; ids, the (source,id) identity, and every verb's addressing are untouched. Beyond the DoD, one behaviour was added that the DoD implied but did not state: _write now renames a legacy-named record on its next update (scanning the dir for another file carrying the same (source,id) and unlinking it) - without this, updating a legacy record would have left two files for one identity; covered by an explicit test. The dir-scan on every write is O(records) but the corpus is small and write-rarely. Stub collisions are harmless ((source,id) stays the key; identical stubs cannot collide on filename because the padded id differs). The 9 live records were migrated with git mv so history follows the rename.
